P,O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322·5662 
Administratian 
322·5660 
Executive/legal 
322·5901 
April 6, 1983 
Robert F. Beaver 
Willard-Brent Company, Inc. 
P.O. Box 11127 
Los Angeles, CA 
90011 
Enfarcement 
322-6441 
Re: 
Advice No. A-83-08l 
Dear Mr. Beaver: 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Commissioner Conrad asked me to provide you with written 
advice on the campaign reporting provisions of the Political 
Reform Act. 
You are the treasurer for two committees 
headquartered in Orange County. 
The committees support 
candidates for local, state and federal offices and they are 
considering supporting the qualification of a state ballot 
measure. 
On March 18, 1983, one of the committees made a 
contribution of $2,500 to a candidate in a city council election 
to be held April 5, 1983.1/ 
You have asked when and where the 
committees must file their campaign statements. 
Both of the committees are "general purpose committees ll 
because they support a number of candidates and/or measures 
being voted upon in different elections.~ General purpose 
committees have the following filing requirements:lI 
1. 
Semi-Annual Statements -
General purpose committees file 
semi-annual statements for each half of the year in which a 
1/ This is the only contribution that has been made this 
year and I am assuming that no other expenditures were made. 
'f:../ 
See the enclosed copy of the "1983 Information Manual 
on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act,lI 
p. 3. 
1/ 
The filing requirements are the same regardless of 
whether candidates or ballot measures are being supported. 


Robert F. Beaver 
April 6, 1983 
Page 2 
committee makes any expenditures or makes or receives any 
contributions.1/ 
2. 
Threshold Statements -
General purpose committees may 
have to file up to four threshold statements a year depending 
upon the level of their campaign activities. 
During 
odd-numbered years, such as 1983, a threshold statement must be 
filed whenever a committee makes contributions or independent 
expenditures of $500 or more during a threshold period.2/ 
During even-numbered years, threshold statements must be filed 
whenever a committee makes or receives contributions of $500 or 
more, or makes expenditures of $500 or more, during the 
threshold period. 
Under the facts you presented: 
1. 
During the first threshold period (January 1 -
March 17), the committees had no campaign activity and, thus, a 
threshold statement did not need to be filed. 
2. 
During the second threshold period (March 18 - May 21), 
a threshold statement will be filed by the committee which made 
the $2,500 contribution because the threshold requirement was 
met. 
The period covered by this statement is January 1 -
May 21. 
No statement must be filed by the other committee. 
3. 
During the remainder of the year, if no additional 
campaign activity occurs, no threshold statements must be 
filed. 
However, the committee which made the $2,500 
contribution must file a semi-annual statement by July 31 
!I The period covered by a semi-annual statement is the 
day after the closing date of the last statement filed (or 
January 1 if no previous statement was filed) -
through June 30 
(1st semi-annual statement), or December 31 (2nd semi-annual 
statement). 
Semi-annual statements are due no later than 
July 31 and January 31. 
If the committees do not make any expenditures or receive 
any contributions during the reporting period, a semi-annual 
statement does not need to be filed. 
2/ 
The threshold periods are January 1 - March 17; 
March 18 -
May 21; July 1 - September 17; and September 18 -
October 22. 


Robert F. Beaver 
April 6, 1983 
Page 3 
because a contribution was made during the first half of the 
year. 
The period covered by this statement will be May 22 -
June 30. 
Any statements filed by the committees will be filed in the 
following places: 
1. 
The original and one copy with the Secretary of 
State, 
2. 
Two copies with the Registrar-Recorder of 
Los Angeles County, 
3. 
Two copies with the Registrar of Voters of the City 
and County of San Francisco, and 
4. 
Two copies in the committees' domicile (Orange 
County) . 
Because the committees are not active wholly within one 
county or city,~1 they do not need to file statements with the 
city clerk of the city in which the April 5 council election 
will be held, unless they are required to do so by local 
ordinance. 
I hope I have answered all of your questions. 
If I can be 
of any further help to you, please do not hesitate to call me at 
(916) 322-5901. 
Very truly yours, 
l:f'f;h~4d?f~ 
Counsel 
Legal Division 
JSM:plh 
cc: 
Commissioner Charles Conrad 
il The committees support candidates running in elections 
outside of one county or city.

actices CommissioIL 
•• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
BARBARA:

322·5901 
March 26, 1983 
enforcement 
322·6441 
Bob Beaver is an old, old friend from my days as a 
Republican Assemblyman, hence he always contacts me on 
campaign reporting matters. 
Would you have one of the staff give him 
a formal 
advice letter, to have in his hot little hand, not only 
to satisfy 
the city clerks mentioned in hi~ letter but 
to protect himself as well as his committee under 
Section 83114 of the FPPC Act. 
Thanks. Please send me a copy of the advice letter 
your staff sent to him. 
See you someday 
Statements of Economic I ntere.! 
322.6444 


WILLARD-BRENT CO. , Inc. 
254 EAST 27th STREET 
• 
POST OFFICE BOX 11127 • LOS ANGELES 90011 
• 
(213) 749-3012 
18, 1983 
Honorable Char~es J. Conrad 
346 Camino Dos Palos 
Thousand Oaks, California 91360 
Dear l'1r. Conrad: 
For some years I have been the treasurer of two on-going 
multI-candidate committees headquartered in Orange County. 
Normally our funds are used to support candidates for state 
legislative office and federal office. 
However. occasionally 
contributions are made to candidates for a city council seat. 
I have no problem complying with the reporting require-
for contributions made to state and federal candidates, 
Today, we are making a contribution of $2,500. to a candi-
date for the city council of an Orange County city where the 
election is to be held on April 5. 
This is the only contribution 
we have made since January 1, 1983. 
In a non-election year, such 
as 1983, our reporting requirements are as of June 30 and Decem-
ber 31. 
does the contribution 
t

On page 11, para C-1-e of the 1982 Information Manual as 
compiled by the FPPC states, "Elected city officials, candidates 
for city office, their controlled committies and committies pri-
marily formed to support or oppose such candidates or ballot 
measures being voted upon within one city must file: ...... with 
the c 
rk of the city." 
Then in a special note, "onc

sections.question: When and where must two general purpose committees headquartered in Orange County file their campaign statements, particularly given that one committee made a $2,500 contribution to a city council candidate on March 18, 1983, for an election to be held April 5, 1983?
sections.conclusion: The filing requirements are as follows: (1) During the first threshold period (January 1 - March 17), no statement was required due to lack of activity; (2) During the second threshold period (March 18 - May 21), a threshold statement covering January 1 - May 21 must be filed by the committee that made the $2,500 contribution; (3) The committee must also file a semi-annual statement by July 31 covering May 22 - June 30. Statements must be filed with the Secretary of State (original plus one copy), Los Angeles County Registrar-Recorder (two copies), San Francisco Registrar of Voters (two copies), and Orange County domicile (two copies). City clerk filings are not required unless mandated by local ordinance.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the filing requirements and locations for campaign statements for two multi-candidate general purpose committees that support candidates across different elections, given a $2,500 contribution to a city council candidate in March 1983?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that the committee making the $2,500 contribution must file a threshold statement for March 18 - May 21 and a semi-annual statement by July 31, with filings to the Secretary of State, Los Angeles County, San Francisco, and Orange County, but not necessarily to the local city clerk unless required by local ordinance.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes incoming request letter dated March 18, 1983 from Robert F. Beaver, internal memo dated March 26, 1983 from Commissioner Conrad requesting formal advice letter, and the formal FPPC advice letter dated April 6, 1983. The analysis extracts from the formal advice letter (A-83-081), which is the primary FPPC response document.

QUESTION: When and where must two general purpose committees headquartered in Orange County file their campaign statements, particularly given that one committee made a $2,500 contribution to a city council candidate on March 18, 1983, for an election to be held April 5, 1983?

CONCLUSION: The filing requirements are as follows: (1) During the first threshold period (January 1 - March 17), no statement was required due to lack of activity; (2) During the second threshold period (March 18 - May 21), a threshold statement covering January 1 - May 21 must be filed by the committee that made the $2,500 contribution; (3) The committee must also file a semi-annual statement by July 31 covering May 22 - June 30. Statements must be filed with the Secretary of State (original plus one copy), Los Angeles County Registrar-Recorder (two copies), San Francisco Registrar of Voters (two copies), and Orange County domicile (two copies). City clerk filings are not required unless mandated by local ordinance.

embedding.summary: This advice letter addresses the campaign reporting requirements for two general purpose committees based in Orange County, responding to questions about filing obligations and locations after one committee contributed $2,500 to a city council candidate on March 18, 1983.
